sourceMovie.title: Angel's Egg
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Angel's Egg (1985) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Mamoru Oshii's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Little Prince
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Angel's Egg clicked for you, The Little Prince (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Angel's Egg?
faq.answer: Try The Little Prince, Children Who Chase Lost Voices, Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle—each shares DNA with Angel's Egg while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Angel's Egg?
faq.answer: Angel's Egg (1985) is best described as Animation, Fantasy, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Angel's Egg worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
